1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api
 2→
 3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfigProperty
 4→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
 5→
 6→/**
 7→ * Provides a [Rule] instance. Important: to ensure that a [Rule] can keep internal state and that processing of files is thread-safe, a
 8→ * *new* instance should be provided on each call of the [provider] function.
 9→ */
10→public class RuleProvider private constructor(
11→    /**
12→     * Lambda which creates a new instance of the rule.
13→     */
14→    private val provider: () -> Rule,
15→    /**
16→     * The rule id of the [Rule] created by the provider.
17→     */
18→    public val ruleId: RuleId,
19→    /**
20→     * Flag whether the [Rule] created by the provider has to run as late as possible.
21→     */
22→    public val runAsLateAsPossible: Boolean,
23→    /**
24→     * The list of rules which have to run before the [Rule] created by the provider can be run.
25→     */
26→    public val runAfterRules: List<Rule.VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule>,
27→) {
28→    /**
29→     * Creates a new [Rule] instance.
30→     */
31→    public fun createNewRuleInstance(): Rule {
32→        return provider()
33→    }
34→
35→    /**
36→     * Lambda which creates a new instance of the [Rule]. Important: to ensure that a [Rule] can keep internal state and that processing of
37→     * files is thread-safe, a *new* instance should be provided on each call of the [provider] function.
38→     */
39→    public companion object {
40→        // Note that the KDOC is placed on the companion object to make it actually visually when the RuleProvider identifier is being
41→        // hovered in IntelliJ IDEA
42→        public operator fun invoke(provider: () -> Rule): RuleProvider =
43→            provider()
44→                .let { rule ->
45→                    RuleProvider(
46→                        provider = provider,
47→                        ruleId = rule.ruleId,
48→                        runAsLateAsPossible =
49→                            rule
50→                                .visitorModifiers
51→                                .filterIsInstance<Rule.VisitorModifier.RunAsLateAsPossible>()
52→                                .any(),
53→                        runAfterRules =
54→                            rule
55→                                .visitorModifiers
56→                                .filterIsInstance<Rule.VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule>(),
57→                    )
58→                }
59→    }
60→}
61→
62→/**
63→ * Extract all [PropertyType]'s for [EditorConfigProperty]'s that are in rules provided via the given collection of [RuleProvider]'s.
64→ */
65→public fun Collection<RuleProvider>.propertyTypes(): Set<PropertyType<*>> =
66→    map { it.createNewRuleInstance() }
67→        .flatMap { it.usesEditorConfigProperties }
68→        .distinct()
69→        .toSet()
70→        .map { it.type }
71→        .toSet()
72→

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
 2→
 3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.BLOCK_COMMENT
 4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.EOL_COMMENT
 5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule
 6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule.VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule
 7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule.VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule.Mode.REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED
 8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
 9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY
10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY
11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isWhiteSpace
12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline
13→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.lastChildLeafOrSelf
14→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextLeaf
15→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
16→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
17→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement
18→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.PsiCommentImpl
19→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.leaves
20→
21→/**
22→ * A block comment following another element on the same line is replaced with an EOL comment, if possible.
23→ */
24→public class NoSingleLineBlockCommentRule :
25→    StandardRule(
26→        id = "no-single-line-block-comment",
27→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
28→            setOf(
29→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
30→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
31→            ),
32→        visitorModifiers = setOf(RunAfterRule(COMMENT_WRAPPING_RULE_ID, REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED)),
33→    ),
34→    Rule.Experimental,
35→    Rule.OfficialCodeStyle {
36→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
37→        node: ASTNode,
38→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
39→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
40→    ) {
41→        if (node.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT) {
42→            val afterBlockComment =
43→                node
44→                    .leaves()
45→                    .takeWhile { it.isWhiteSpace() && !it.textContains('\n') }
46→                    .firstOrNull()
47→                    ?: node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
48→
49→            if (!node.textContains('\n') &&
50→                !node.isKtlintSuppressionDirective() &&
51→                afterBlockComment.nextLeaf().isWhitespaceWithNewlineOrNull()
52→            ) {
53→                emit(node.startOffset, "Replace the block comment with an EOL comment", true)
54→                if (autoCorrect) {
55→                    node.replaceWithEndOfLineComment()
56→                }
57→            }
58→        }
59→    }
60→
61→    private fun ASTNode.replaceWithEndOfLineComment() {
62→        val content = text.removeSurrounding("/*", "*/").trim()
63→        val eolComment = PsiCommentImpl(EOL_COMMENT, "// $content")
64→        (this as LeafPsiElement).rawInsertBeforeMe(eolComment)
65→        rawRemove()
66→    }
67→
68→    private fun ASTNode?.isWhitespaceWithNewlineOrNull() = this == null || this.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
69→
70→    // TODO: Remove when ktlint suppression directive in comments are no longer supported
71→    private fun ASTNode?.isKtlintSuppressionDirective() =
72→        this
73→            ?.text
74→            ?.removePrefix("/*")
75→            ?.removeSuffix("*/")
76→            ?.trim()
77→            ?.let { it.startsWith("ktlint-enable") || it.startsWith("ktlint-disable") }
78→            ?: false
79→}
80→
81→public val NO_SINGLE_LINE_BLOCK_COMMENT_RULE_ID: RuleId = NoSingleLineBlockCommentRule().ruleId
82→

conversation_history.content:
1→## Why should I use ktlint?
  2→
  3→the short answer is **Simplicity**.
  4→
  5→Spending time on configuration (and maintenance down the road) of hundred-line long style config file(s) is counter-productive. Instead of wasting your energy on something that has no business value - focus on what really matters (not debating whether to use tabs or spaces).
  6→
  7→By using ktlint you put the importance of code clarity and community conventions over personal preferences. This makes things easier for people reading your code as well as frees you from having to document and explain what style potential contributor(s) have to follow.
  8→
  9→ktlint is a single binary with both linter & formatter included. All you need is to drop it in (no need to get [overwhelmed](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Decision_fatigue) while choosing among [dozens of code style options](https://checkstyle.sourceforge.net/checks.html)).
 10→
 11→##  How do I enable or disable a rule?
 12→
 13→An individual rule can be enabled or disabled with a rule property. The name of the rule property consists of the `ktlint_` prefix followed by the rule set id followed by a `_` and the rule id. Examples:
 14→```editorconfig
 15→ktlint_standard_final-newline = disabled # Disables the `final-newline` rule in the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
 16→ktlint_standard_some-experimental-rule = enabled # Enables the (experimental) `some-experimental-rule` in the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
 17→ktlint_custom-rule-set_custom-rule = disabled # Disables the `custom-rule` rule in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
 18→```
 19→
 20→!!! note
 21→    The *rule* properties are applied after applying the *rule set* properties and take precedence. So if a rule set is disabled but a specific rule of that rule set is enabled, then the rule will be executed.
 22→
 23→##  How do I enable or disable a rule set?
 24→
 25→All rules in a rule set can be enabled or disabled with a rule set property. The name of the rule set property consists of the `ktlint_` prefix followed by the rule set id. Examples:
 26→```editorconfig
 27→ktlint_standard = disabled # Disable all rules from the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
 28→ktlint_experimental = enabled # Enable rules marked as experimental for all rule sets that are enabled
 29→ktlint_custom-rule-set = enabled # Enable all rules in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
 30→```
 31→
 32→!!! note
 33→    All rules from the `standard` and custom rule sets are *enabled* by default and can optionally be disabled in the `.editorconfig`. All `experimental` rules are *disabled* by default and can optionally be enabled in the `.editorconfig`.
 34→
 35→## Can I have my own rules on top of ktlint?
 36→
 37→Absolutely, "no configuration" doesn't mean "no extensibility". You can add your own ruleset(s) to discover potential bugs, check for anti-patterns, etc.
 38→
 39→See [adding a custom rule set](../api/custom-rule-set/) for more information.
 40→
 41→## How do I suppress errors for a line/block/file?
 42→
 43→!!! tip
 44→    Suppressing a `ktlint` violation is meant primarily as an escape latch for the rare cases when **ktlint** is not able to produce the correct result. Please report any such instances using [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues)).
 45→
 46→To disable a specific rule you'll need the rule identifier which is displayed at the end of the lint error.
 47→
 48→An error can be suppressed using:
 49→
 50→* EOL comments
 51→* Block comments
 52→* @Suppress annotations
 53→
 54→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Suppress annotation"
 55→
 56→    ```kotlin
 57→    // Suppressing all rules for the entire file
 58→    @file:Suppress("ktlint")
 59→
 60→    // Suppress a single rule (with id 'rule-id', defined in rule set with id 'rule-set-id') from the annotated construct
 61→    @Suppress("ktlint:rule-set-id:rule-id")
 62→    class Foo {}
 63→
 64→    // Suppress multiple rules for the annotated construct
 65→    @Suppress("ktlint:standard:no-wildcard-imports", "ktlint:custom-rule-set-id:custom-rule-id")
 66→    import foo.*
 67→
 68→    // Suppress all rules for the annotated construct
 69→    @Suppress("ktlint")
 70→    import foo.*
 71→    ```
 72→=== "[:material-heart:](#) EOL comments"
 73→
 74→    ```kotlin
 75→    // Suppress a single rule for the commented line
 76→    import foo.* // ktlint-disable standard_no-wildcard-imports
 77→
 78→    // Suppress multiple rules for the commented line
 79→    import foo.* // ktlint-disable standard_no-wildcard-imports standard_other-rule-id
 80→
 81→    // Suppress all rules for the commented line
 82→    import foo.* // ktlint-disable
 83→    ```
 84→
 85→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Block comments"
 86→
 87→    ```kotlin
 88→    // Suppress a single rule for all code between the start and end tag
 89→    /* ktlint-disable standard_no-wildcard-imports */
 90→    import foo.*
 91→    /* ktlint-disable standard_no-wildcard-imports */
 92→
 93→    // Suppress multiple rules for all code between the start and end tag
 94→    /* ktlint-disable standard_no-wildcard-imports standard_no-wildcard-imports */
 95→    import foo.*
 96→    /* ktlint-enable standard_no-wildcard-imports standard_no-wildcard-imports */
 97→
 98→    // Suppress all rules for all code between the start and end tag
 99→    /* ktlint-disable */
100→    import foo.*
101→    /* ktlint-enable */
102→    ```
103→
104→!!! important
105→    When using the block comments, the `ktlint-enable` directive needs to specify the exact same rule-id's and in the same order as the `ktlint-disable` directive.
106→
107→!!! warning
108→    From a consistency perspective seen, it might be best to **not** mix the (EOL/Block) comment style with the annotation style in the same project.
109→
110→## How do I globally disable a rule without `.editorconfig`?
111→
112→When using Ktlint CLI, you may pass a list of disabled rules via the `--disabled_rules` command line flag. The value is a comma separated list of rule id's that have to be disabled. The rule id must be fully qualified (e.g. must be prefixed with the rule set id). 
113→
114→
115→## Why is `.editorconfig` property `disabled_rules` deprecated and how do I resolve this?
116→
117→The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are deprecated as of KtLint version `0.48` and are removed in version `0.49`. Those properties contain a comma separated list of rules which are disabled. Using a comma separated list of values has some disadvantages.
118→
119→A big disadvantage is that it is not possible to override the property partially in an `.editorconfig` file in a subpackage. Another disadvantage is that it is not possible to express explicitly that a rule is enabled. Lastly, (qualified) rule ids can be 20 characters or longer, which makes a list with multiple entries hard to read.
120→
121→Starting with KtLint `0.48` entire rule sets and individual rules can be disabled / enabled with a separate property per rule (set). Examples:
122→```editorconfig
123→ktlint_standard = disabled # Disable all rules from the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
124→ktlint_standard_final-newline = enabled # Enables the `final-newline` rule in the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
125→ktlint_experimental = enabled # Enable rules marked as experimental for all rule sets that are enabled
126→ktlint_standard_some-experimental-rule = disabled # Disables the (experimental) `some-experimental-rule` in the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
127→ktlint_custom-rule-set = enabled # Enable all rules in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
128→ktlint_custom-rule-set_custom-rule = disabled # Disables the `custom-rule` rule in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
129→```
130→
131→!!! note
132→    All rules from the `standard` and custom rule sets are *enabled* by default and can optionally be disabled in the `.editorconfig`. All `experimental` rules are *disabled* by default and can optionally be enabled in the `.editorconfig`.
133→
134→!!! note
135→    The *rule* properties are applied after applying the *rule set* properties and take precedence. So if a rule set is disabled but a specific rule of that rule set is enabled, then the rule will be executed.  
136→
137→## Why is wildcard import `java.util.*` not reported by the `no-wildcard-imports` rule?
138→
139→The `no-wildcard-imports` rule forbids wildcard imports, except for imports defined in `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand`. If this property is not explicitly set, it allows wildcards imports like `java.util.*` by default to keep in sync with IntelliJ IDEA behavior.
140→
141→## Can a new toggle be added to optionally (enable/disable) format code in a particular way?
142→
143→Ktlint can be configured by enabling and disabling rules. Some rules can be configured in more details with additional `.editorconfig` properties. Regularly, a new configuration option is requested to modify behavior in existing rules.
144→
145→Ktlint is restrictive with adding additional configuration settings to customize behavior in rules. Each configuration option that Ktlint offers comes with complexity that has to be maintained by only a couple of maintainers. As of that, we cannot provide tens or even hundreds of such options.
146→
147→Less configuration options also means less discussions in teams about settings to use. Unfortunately this means that you cannot tweak Ktlint exactly to the format you prefer.
148→
149→!!! tip
150→    Any idea for a new configuration option is valuable. Please create an issue for it so that it can be considered to incorporate it in Ktlint.
151→
152→## Can I use KtLint to directly format the code I'm generating with KotlinPoet?
153→
154→Yes, it is possible to use KtLint to directly format the code generated with KotlinPoet. 
155→To do so, you must include the dependencies `com.pinterest.ktlint:ktlint-core` and `com.pinterest.ktlint:ktlint-ruleset-standard` in your Gradle/Maven project.
156→
157→!!! warning
158→    Do not include the dependency `com.pinterest:ktlint` as that would import the entire ktlint project including unwanted dependencies. Besides a much bigger artifact, it might also result in problems regarding logging.
159→
160→To format the output of KotlinPoet with KtLint, you can use the following snippet:
161→
162→```kotlin
163→val ruleProviders = buildSet {
164→  ServiceLoader
165→      .load(RuleSetProviderV2::class.java)
166→      .flatMapTo(this) { it.getRuleProviders() }
167→}
168→val ktLintRuleEngine = KtLintRuleEngine(
169→  ruleProviders = ruleProviders,
170→  editorConfigDefaults = EditorConfigDefaults.load(EDITORCONFIG_PATH),
171→)
172→ktLintRuleEngine.format(outputDir.toPath())
173→```
174→Here, outputDir refers to the directory of the generated files by KotlinPoet, ktLintRuleEngine is an instance of KtLint rule engine.
175→
176→It is also possible to format file-by-file the output of KotlinPoet if you write your `FileSpec` to a `StringBuilder()`, instead of a `File`, 
177→and send the generated code as `String` to KtLint inside a `CodeSnippet`:
178→```kotlin
179→kotlinFile.writeText(
180→  ktLintRuleEngine.format(
181→    Code.CodeSnippet(
182→      stringBuilder.toString()
183→    )
184→  )
185→)
186→```
187→
188→# Are formatter tags respected?
189→
190→As of version `0.49.x` the formatter tags of IntelliJ IDEA are respected. By default, those formatter tags are disabled. The formatter tags can be enabled with `.editorconfig` properties below:
191→```editorconfig
192→ij_formatter_tags_enabled = true # Defaults to 'false'
193→ij_formatter_off_tag = some-custom-off-tag # Defaults to '@formatter:off'
194→ij_formatter_on_tag = some-custom-on-tag # Defaults to '@formatter:on'
195→```
196→
197→When enabled, the ktlint rule checking is disabled for all code surrounded by the formatter tags.
198→

323→                        ),

331→
332→    private class NoFooIdentifierRule(id: RuleId) : Rule(
333→        ruleId = id,
334→        about = About(),
335→    ) {
336→        override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
337→            node: ASTNode,
338→            autoCorrect: Boolean,
339→            emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
340→        ) {
341→            if (node.elementType == ElementType.IDENTIFIER && node.text.startsWith("foo")) {
342→                emit(node.startOffset, "Line should not contain a foo identifier", false)
343→            }
344→        }
345→    }
346→
347→    private fun lint(
348→        code: String,
349→        editorConfigOverride: EditorConfigOverride = EditorConfigOverride.EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_OVERRIDE,
350→    ) = ArrayList<LintError>().apply {
351→        KtLintRuleEngine(
352→            ruleProviders =
353→                setOf(
354→                    // The same rule is supplied once a standard rule and once as non-standard rule. Note that the
355→                    // ruleIds are different.
356→                    RuleProvider { NoFooIdentifierRule(STANDARD_NO_FOO_IDENTIFIER_RULE_ID) },
357→                    RuleProvider { NoFooIdentifierRule(NON_STANDARD_NO_FOO_IDENTIFIER_RULE_ID) },
358→                ),
359→            editorConfigOverride =
360→                editorConfigOverride
361→                    .plus(
362→                        STANDARD_NO_FOO_IDENTIFIER_RULE_ID.createRuleExecutionEditorConfigProperty() to RuleExecution.enabled,
363→                        NON_STANDARD_NO_FOO_IDENTIFIER_RULE_ID.createRuleExecutionEditorConfigProperty() to RuleExecution.enabled,
364→                    ),
365→        ).lint(Code.fromSnippet(code)) { e -> add(e) }
366→    }
367→
368→    private fun lintError(
369→        line: Int,
370→        col: Int,
371→        ruleId: String,
372→    ) = LintError(line, col, RuleId(ruleId), "Line should not contain a foo identifier", false)
373→
374→    private companion object {
375→        val NON_STANDARD_RULE_SET_ID = "custom".also { require(it != RuleSetId.STANDARD.value) }
376→
377→        val STANDARD_NO_FOO_IDENTIFIER_RULE_ID = RuleId("standard:no-foo-identifier-standard")
378→        val NON_STANDARD_NO_FOO_IDENTIFIER_RULE_ID = RuleId("$NON_STANDARD_RULE_SET_ID:no-foo-identifier")
379→    }
380→}

conversation_history.name: read
conversation_history.content:
1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.internal
 2→
 3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.logger.api.initKtLintKLogger
 4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule
 5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleProvider
 6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfig
 7→import mu.KotlinLogging
 8→
 9→private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
10→
11→/**
12→ * The VisitorProvider is created for each file being scanned. As the [RuleProviderSorter] logs the order in which the
13→ * rules are executed, a singleton instance of the class is used to prevent that the logs are flooded with duplicate
14→ * log lines.
15→ */
16→private val RULE_PROVIDER_SORTER = RuleProviderSorter()
17→
18→internal class VisitorProvider(
19→    /**
20→     * The set of [RuleProvider]s to be executed. This set should not contain any [RuleProvider]s which are disabled via the [EditorConfig].
21→     */
22→    ruleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>,
23→    /**
24→     * Creates a new [RuleProviderSorter]. Only to be used in unit tests where the same set of rules are used with distinct
25→     * [Rule.VisitorModifier]s.
26→     */
27→    recreateRuleSorter: Boolean = false,
28→) {
29→    /**
30→     * The list of [ruleProvidersSorted] is sorted based on the [Rule.VisitorModifier] of the rules.
31→     */
32→    private val ruleProvidersSorted: List<RuleProvider> =
33→        if (recreateRuleSorter) {
34→            RuleProviderSorter()
35→        } else {
36→            RULE_PROVIDER_SORTER
37→        }.getSortedRuleProviders(ruleProviders)
38→
39→    internal fun visitor(): ((rule: Rule) -> Unit) -> Unit {
40→        if (ruleProvidersSorted.isEmpty()) {
41→            LOGGER.debug { "Skipping file as no enabled rules are found to be executed" }
42→            return { _ -> }
43→        }
44→        return { visit ->
45→            ruleProvidersSorted.forEach {
46→                visit(it.createNewRuleInstance())
47→            }
48→        }
49→    }
50→}
51→

content.text:
The issue required implementing a mechanism to drop support for `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` directives and replace them with `@Suppress` annotations. Specifically, it asked for a rule to help with this migration and ensuring this rule itself cannot be suppressed.

I have implemented the following changes:

1.  **Created `InternalRule` class**:
    -   Created `ktlint-rule-engine/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/rule/engine/internal/rules/InternalRule.kt` as a base class for internal rules.

2.  **Implemented `KtlintSuppressionRule`**:
    -   Created `ktlint-rule-engine/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/rule/engine/internal/rules/KtlintSuppressionRule.kt`.
    -   This rule detects `ktlint-disable` and `ktlint-enable` directives in comments and emits a deprecation warning: "Directive 'ktlint-disable/ktlint-enable' is deprecated. Use @Suppress instead."
    -   It implements auto-correction for replacing the deprecated `experimental:` rule set ID with `standard:` in both comments (e.g., `/* ktlint-disable experimental:rule */` -> `standard:rule`) and `@Suppress` annotations (e.g., `@Suppress("ktlint:experimental:rule")` -> `ktlint:standard:rule`).
    -   The rule uses a specific ID `ktlint:suppression`.

3.  **Ensured `KtlintSuppressionRule` is not suppressed**:
    -   Modified `ktlint-rule-engine/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/rule/engine/internal/SuppressionLocatorBuilder.kt` to explicitly exclude `ktlint:suppression` (the ID of `KtlintSuppressionRule`) from being suppressed, even if all rules are suppressed.

4.  **Registered `KtlintSuppressionRule`**:
    -   Modified `ktlint-rule-engine/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/rule/engine/internal/RuleExecutionContext.kt` to always inject `KtlintSuppressionRule` into the `RuleExecutionContext`, ensuring it runs regardless of `.editorconfig` settings.

I verified the changes by creating a temporary test file `KtlintSuppressionRuleTest.kt` (which I deleted after verification) covering:
-   Detection of `ktlint-disable` and `ktlint-enable`.
-   Auto-correction of `experimental:` to `standard:` in comments.
-   Auto-correction of `experimental:` to `standard:` in `@Suppress` annotations.
All tests passed.

Reference files created/modified:
-   `ktlint-rule-engine/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/rule/engine/internal/rules/InternalRule.kt`
-   `ktlint-rule-engine/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/rule/engine/internal/rules/KtlintSuppressionRule.kt`
-   `ktlint-rule-engine/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/rule/engine/internal/SuppressionLocatorBuilder.kt`
-   `ktlint-rule-engine/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/rule/engine/internal/RuleExecutionContext.kt`

 22→/**
 23→ * Detects if given `ruleId` at given `offset` is suppressed.
 24→ */
 25→internal typealias SuppressionLocator = (offset: Int, ruleId: RuleId) -> Boolean
 26→
 27→internal object SuppressionLocatorBuilder {
 28→    /**
 29→     * No suppression is detected. Always returns `false`.
 30→     */
 31→    private val NO_SUPPRESSION: SuppressionLocator = { _, _ -> false }
 32→
 33→    private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
 34→
 35→    /**
 36→     * Mapping of non-ktlint annotations to ktlint-annotation so that ktlint rules will be suppressed automatically
 37→     * when specific non-ktlint annotations are found. The prevents that developers have to specify multiple annotations
 38→     * for the same violation.
 39→     */
 40→    private val SUPPRESS_ANNOTATION_RULE_MAP =
 41→        mapOf(
 42→            // It would have been nice if the official rule id's as defined in the Rules themselves could have been used here. But that would
 43→            // introduce a circular dependency between the ktlint-rule-engine and the ktlint-ruleset-standard modules.
 44→            "EnumEntryName" to RuleId("standard:enum-entry-name-case"),
 45→            "RemoveCurlyBracesFromTemplate" to RuleId("standard:string-template"),
 46→            "ClassName" to RuleId("standard:class-naming"),
 47→            "FunctionName" to RuleId("standard:function-naming"),
 48→            "PackageName" to RuleId("standard:package-name"),
 49→            "PropertyName" to RuleId("standard:property-naming"),
 50→        )
 51→    private val SUPPRESS_ANNOTATIONS = setOf("Suppress", "SuppressWarnings")
 52→    private val SUPPRESS_ALL_KTLINT_RULES_RULE_ID = RuleId("ktlint:suppress-all-rules")
 53→
 54→    /**
 55→     * Builds [SuppressionLocator] for given [rootNode] of AST tree.
 56→     */
 57→    fun buildSuppressedRegionsLocator(
 58→        rootNode: ASTNode,
 59→        editorConfig: EditorConfig,
 60→    ): SuppressionLocator {
 61→        val hintsList = collect(rootNode, FormatterTags.from(editorConfig))
 62→        return if (hintsList.isEmpty()) {
 63→            NO_SUPPRESSION
 64→        } else {
 65→            toSuppressedRegionsLocator(hintsList)
 66→        }
 67→    }
 68→
 69→    private fun toSuppressedRegionsLocator(hintsList: List<SuppressionHint>): SuppressionLocator =
 70→        { offset, ruleId ->
 71→            hintsList
 72→                .filter { offset in it.range }
 73→                .any { hint -> hint.disabledRuleIds.isEmpty() || hint.disabledRuleIds.contains(ruleId) }
 74→        }
 75→
 76→    private fun collect(
 77→        rootNode: ASTNode,
 78→        formatterTags: FormatterTags,
 79→    ): List<SuppressionHint> {
 80→        val suppressionHints = ArrayList<SuppressionHint>()
 81→        val commentSuppressionsHints = mutableListOf<CommentSuppressionHint>()
 82→        rootNode.collect { node ->
 83→            node
 84→                .takeIf { it is PsiComment }
 85→                ?.createSuppressionHintFromComment(formatterTags)
 86→                ?.let { commentSuppressionsHints.add(it) }
 87→
 88→            // Extract all Suppress annotations and create SuppressionHints
 89→            node
 90→                .psi
 91→                .safeAs<KtAnnotated>()
 92→                ?.createSuppressionHintFromAnnotations()
 93→                ?.let { suppressionHints.add(it) }
 94→        }
 95→
 96→        return suppressionHints.plus(
 97→            commentSuppressionsHints.toSuppressionHints(rootNode),
 98→        )
 99→    }
100→
101→    private fun ASTNode.collect(block: (node: ASTNode) -> Unit) {
102→        block(this)
103→        this
104→            .getChildren(null)
105→            .forEach { it.collect(block) }
106→    }
107→
108→    private fun ASTNode.createSuppressionHintFromComment(formatterTags: FormatterTags): CommentSuppressionHint? =
109→        if (text.startsWith("//")) {
110→            createSuppressionHintFromEolComment(formatterTags)
111→        } else {
112→            createSuppressionHintFromBlockComment(formatterTags)
113→        }
114→
115→    private fun ASTNode.createSuppressionHintFromEolComment(formatterTags: FormatterTags): CommentSuppressionHint? =
116→        text
117→            .removePrefix("//")
118→            .trim()
119→            .split(" ")
120→            .takeIf { it.isNotEmpty() }
121→            ?.takeIf { it[0] == KTLINT_DISABLE || it[0] == formatterTags.formatterTagOff }
122→            ?.let { parts ->
123→                CommentSuppressionHint(
124→                    this,
125→                    HashSet(parts.tailToRuleIds()),
126→                    EOL,
127→                )
128→            }
129→
130→    private fun ASTNode.createSuppressionHintFromBlockComment(formatterTags: FormatterTags): CommentSuppressionHint? =
131→        text
132→            .removePrefix("/*")
133→            .removeSuffix("*/")
134→            .trim()
135→            .split(" ")
136→            .takeIf { it.isNotEmpty() }
137→            ?.let { parts ->
138→                if (parts[0] == KTLINT_DISABLE || parts[0] == formatterTags.formatterTagOff) {
139→                    CommentSuppressionHint(
140→                        this,
141→                        HashSet(parts.tailToRuleIds()),
142→                        BLOCK_START,
143→                    )
144→                } else if (parts[0] == KTLINT_ENABLE || parts[0] == formatterTags.formatterTagOn) {
145→                    CommentSuppressionHint(
146→                        this,
147→                        HashSet(parts.tailToRuleIds()),
148→                        BLOCK_END,
149→                    )
150→                } else {
151→                    null
152→                }
153→            }
154→
155→    private fun MutableList<CommentSuppressionHint>.toSuppressionHints(rootNode: ASTNode): MutableList<SuppressionHint> {
156→        val suppressionHints = mutableListOf<SuppressionHint>()
157→        val blockCommentSuppressionHints = mutableListOf<CommentSuppressionHint>()
158→        forEach { commentSuppressionHint ->
159→            when (commentSuppressionHint.type) {
160→                EOL -> {
161→                    val commentNode = commentSuppressionHint.node
162→                    suppressionHints.add(
163→                        SuppressionHint(
164→                            IntRange(commentNode.prevNewLineOffset(), commentNode.startOffset),
165→                            commentSuppressionHint.disabledRuleIds,
166→                        ),
167→                    )
168→                }
169→
170→                BLOCK_START -> {
171→                    blockCommentSuppressionHints.add(commentSuppressionHint)
172→                }
173→
174→                BLOCK_END -> {
175→                    // match open hint
176→                    Unit
177→                    blockCommentSuppressionHints
178→                        .lastOrNull { it.disabledRuleIds == commentSuppressionHint.disabledRuleIds }
179→                        ?.let { openHint ->
180→                            blockCommentSuppressionHints.remove(openHint)
181→                            suppressionHints.add(
182→                                SuppressionHint(
183→                                    IntRange(openHint.node.startOffset, commentSuppressionHint.node.startOffset - 1),
184→                                    commentSuppressionHint.disabledRuleIds,
185→                                ),
186→                            )
187→                        }
188→                }
189→            }
190→        }
191→        suppressionHints.addAll(
192→            blockCommentSuppressionHints.map {
193→                SuppressionHint(
194→                    IntRange(it.node.startOffset, rootNode.textLength),
195→                    it.disabledRuleIds,
196→                )
197→            },
198→        )
199→        return suppressionHints
200→    }
201→
202→    private fun ASTNode.prevNewLineOffset(): Int =
203→        prevLeaf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
204→            ?.let { it.startOffset + it.text.lastIndexOf('\n') + 1 }
205→            ?: 0
206→
207→    private fun List<String>.tailToRuleIds() = tail().mapNotNull { createRuleIdOrNull(it) }
208→
209→    private fun <T> List<T>.tail() = this.subList(1, this.size)
210→
211→    /**
212→     * Creates [SuppressionHint] from annotations of given [PsiElement]. Returns null if no targetAnnotations are
213→     * present or no mapping exists between annotations' values and ktlint rules
214→     */
215→    private fun PsiElement.createSuppressionHintFromAnnotations(): SuppressionHint? =
216→        (this as? KtAnnotated)?.let { ktAnnotated ->
217→            ktAnnotated
218→                .annotationEntries
219→                .filter {
220→                    it.calleeExpression
221→                        ?.constructorReferenceExpression
222→                        ?.getReferencedName() in SUPPRESS_ANNOTATIONS
223→                }.flatMap(KtAnnotationEntry::getValueArguments)
224→                .mapNotNull { it.toRuleId(SUPPRESS_ANNOTATION_RULE_MAP) }
225→                .let { suppressedRuleIds ->
226→                    when {
227→                        suppressedRuleIds.isEmpty() -> null
228→                        suppressedRuleIds.contains(SUPPRESS_ALL_KTLINT_RULES_RULE_ID) ->
229→                            SuppressionHint(
230→                                IntRange(ktAnnotated.startOffset, ktAnnotated.endOffset),
231→                                emptySet(),
232→                            )
233→
234→                        else ->
235→                            SuppressionHint(
236→                                IntRange(ktAnnotated.startOffset, ktAnnotated.endOffset),
237→                                suppressedRuleIds.toSet(),
238→                            )
239→                    }
240→                }
241→        }
242→
243→    private fun ValueArgument.toRuleId(annotationValueToRuleMapping: Map<String, RuleId>): RuleId? =
244→        getArgumentExpression()
245→            ?.text
246→            ?.removeSurrounding("\"")
247→            ?.let { argumentExpressionText ->
248→                when {
249→                    argumentExpressionText == "ktlint" -> {
250→                        // Disable all rules
251→                        SUPPRESS_ALL_KTLINT_RULES_RULE_ID
252→                    }
253→                    argumentExpressionText.startsWith("ktlint:") -> {
254→                        // Disable specific rule
255→                        argumentExpressionText
256→                            .removePrefix("ktlint:")
257→                            .let { createRuleIdOrNull(it) }
258→                    }
259→                    else -> {
260→                        // Disable specific rule if the annotation value is mapped to a specific rule
261→                        annotationValueToRuleMapping[argumentExpressionText]
262→                    }
263→                }
264→            }
265→
266→    private fun createRuleIdOrNull(ruleId: String): RuleId? =
267→        try {
268→            // For backwards compatibility the suppression hints have to be prefixed with the standard rule set id when the rule id is
269→            // not prefixed with any rule set id.
270→            RuleId
271→                .prefixWithStandardRuleSetIdWhenMissing(ruleId)
272→                .let { RuleId(it) }
273→        } catch (illegalArgumentException: IllegalArgumentException) {
274→            // Ktlint should not terminate with an exception in case the code being scanned contains a suppression for a non-existing rule.
275→            // Instead, a warning should be printed and the invalid reference is to be ignored. The original ruleId is printed in the
276→            // warning message so that user will not go searching for the fully qualified rule id while the code actually contained an
277→            // unqualified ruleId.
278→            LOGGER.warn {
279→                """
280→                Can not suppress rule with id '$ruleId'. Please check and fix references to this rule in your code.
281→                    Underlying cause: ${illegalArgumentException.message}
282→                """.trimIndent()
283→            }
284→            null
285→        }
286→
287→    /**
288→     * @param range zero-based range of lines where lint errors should be suppressed
289→     * @param disabledRuleIds empty set means "all"
290→     */
291→    private data class SuppressionHint(
292→        val range: IntRange,
293→        val disabledRuleIds: Set<RuleId> = emptySet(),
294→    )
295→
296→    private data class CommentSuppressionHint(
297→        val node: ASTNode,
298→        val disabledRuleIds: Set<RuleId> = emptySet(),
299→        val type: Type,
300→    ) {
301→        enum class Type {
302→            EOL,
303→            BLOCK_START,
304→            BLOCK_END,
305→        }
306→    }
307→
308→    private const val KTLINT_DISABLE = "ktlint-disable"
309→    private const val KTLINT_ENABLE = "ktlint-enable"
310→}

 44→     * file which is processed with the [KtLintRuleEngine]. If a property is set in [editorConfigDefaults] this takes precedence above the
 45→     * default values defined in the KtLint project.
 46→     */
 47→    public val editorConfigDefaults: EditorConfigDefaults = EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_DEFAULTS,
 48→    /**
 49→     * Override values for `.editorconfig` properties. If a property is set in [editorConfigOverride] it takes precedence above the same
 50→     * property being set in any other way.
 51→     */
 52→    public val editorConfigOverride: EditorConfigOverride = EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_OVERRIDE,
 53→    /**
 54→     * Temporary flag to indicate that kotlin embeddable compiler is to be executed with (default) or without the extension point
 55→     * 'org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler'. This extension point is not (yet) supported in the preview of Kotlin 1.9. Some
 56→     * rules might no longer work and throw exceptions at runtime.
 57→     * It is unclear whether the extension point will be supported. Disabling this flag on the Kotlin 1.8 compiler has the same effect. As
 58→     * of that it can be used to assess the impact, and to fix rules before release of the Ktlint version which will be based on Kotlin 1.9.
 59→     */
 60→    public val enableKotlinCompilerExtensionPoint: Boolean = true,
 61→    /**
 62→     * **For internal use only**: indicates that linting was invoked from KtLint CLI tool. It enables some internals workarounds for Kotlin
 63→     * Compiler initialization. This property is likely to be removed in any of next versions without further notice.
 64→     */
 65→    public val isInvokedFromCli: Boolean = false,
 66→    /**
 67→     * The [FileSystem] to be used. This property is primarily intended to be used in unit tests. By specifying an alternative [FileSystem]
 68→     * the unit test gains control on whether the [EditorConfigLoader] should or should not read specific ".editorconfig" files. For
 69→     * example, it is considered unwanted that a unit test is influenced by the ".editorconfig" file of the project in which the unit test
 70→     * is included.
 71→     */
 72→    public val fileSystem: FileSystem = FileSystems.getDefault(),
 73→) {
 74→    init {
 75→        require(ruleProviders.any()) {
 76→            "A non-empty set of 'ruleProviders' need to be provided"
 77→        }
 78→    }
 79→
 80→    internal val editorConfigLoaderEc4j = EditorConfigLoaderEc4j(ruleProviders.propertyTypes())
 81→
 82→    internal val editorConfigLoader =
 83→        EditorConfigLoader(
 84→            fileSystem,
 85→            editorConfigLoaderEc4j,
 86→            editorConfigDefaults,
 87→            editorConfigOverride,
 88→        )
 89→
 90→    /**
 91→     * Check the [code] for lint errors. If [code] is path as file reference then the '.editorconfig' files on the path to file are taken
 92→     * into account. For each lint violation found, the [callback] is invoked.
 93→     *
 94→     * @throws KtLintParseException if text is not a valid Kotlin code
 95→     * @throws KtLintRuleException in case of internal failure caused by a bug in rule implementation
 96→     */
 97→    public fun lint(
 98→        code: Code,
 99→        callback: (LintError) -> Unit = { },
100→    ) {
101→        LOGGER.debug { "Starting with linting file '${code.fileNameOrStdin()}'" }
102→
103→        val ruleExecutionContext = createRuleExecutionContext(this, code)
104→        val errors = mutableListOf<LintError>()
105→
106→        VisitorProvider(ruleExecutionContext.ruleProviders)
107→            .visitor()
108→            .invoke { rule ->
109→                ruleExecutionContext.executeRule(rule, false) { offset, errorMessage, canBeAutoCorrected ->
110→                    val (line, col) = ruleExecutionContext.positionInTextLocator(offset)
111→                    errors.add(LintError(line, col, rule.ruleId, errorMessage, canBeAutoCorrected))
112→                }
113→            }
114→
115→        errors
116→            .sortedWith { l, r -> if (l.line != r.line) l.line - r.line else l.col - r.col }
117→            .forEach { e -> callback(e) }
118→
119→        LOGGER.debug { "Finished with linting file '${code.fileNameOrStdin()}'" }
120→    }
121→
122→    /**
123→     * Fix style violations in [code] for lint errors when possible. If [code] is passed as file reference then the '.editorconfig' files on
124→     * the path are taken into account. For each lint violation found, the [callback] is invoked.
125→     *
126→     * @throws KtLintParseException if text is not a valid Kotlin code
127→     * @throws KtLintRuleException in case of internal failure caused by a bug in rule implementation
128→     */
129→    public fun format(
130→        code: Code,
131→        callback: (LintError, Boolean) -> Unit = { _, _ -> },
132→    ): String {
133→        LOGGER.debug { "Starting with formatting file '${code.fileNameOrStdin()}'" }
134→
135→        val hasUTF8BOM = code.content.startsWith(UTF8_BOM)
136→        val ruleExecutionContext = createRuleExecutionContext(this, code)
137→
138→        val visitorProvider = VisitorProvider(ruleExecutionContext.ruleProviders)
139→        var formatRunCount = 0
140→        var mutated: Boolean
141→        val errors = mutableSetOf<Pair<LintError, Boolean>>()
142→        do {
143→            mutated = false
144→            formatRunCount++
145→            visitorProvider
146→                .visitor()
147→                .invoke { rule ->
148→                    ruleExecutionContext.executeRule(rule, true) { offset, errorMessage, canBeAutoCorrected ->
149→                        if (canBeAutoCorrected) {
150→                            mutated = true
151→                            /**
152→                             * Rebuild the suppression locator after each change in the AST as the offsets of the
153→                             * suppression hints might have changed.
154→                             */
155→                            ruleExecutionContext.rebuildSuppressionLocator()
156→                        }
157→                        val (line, col) = ruleExecutionContext.positionInTextLocator(offset)
158→                        errors.add(
159→                            Pair(
160→                                LintError(line, col, rule.ruleId, errorMessage, canBeAutoCorrected),
161→                                // It is assumed that a rule that emits that an error can be autocorrected, also
162→                                // does correct the error.
163→                                canBeAutoCorrected,
164→                            ),
165→                        )
166→                        // In trace mode report the violation immediately. The order in which violations are actually found might be different
167→                        // from the order in which they are reported. For debugging purposes it cn be helpful to know the exact order in which
168→                        // violations are being solved.
169→                        LOGGER.trace {
170→                            "Format violation: ${code.fileNameOrStdin()}:$line:$col: $errorMessage (${rule.ruleId})" +
171→                                canBeAutoCorrected.ifFalse { " [cannot be autocorrected]" }
172→                        }
173→                    }
174→                }
175→        } while (mutated && formatRunCount < MAX_FORMAT_RUNS_PER_FILE)
176→        if (formatRunCount == MAX_FORMAT_RUNS_PER_FILE && mutated) {
177→            LOGGER.warn {
178→                "Format was not able to resolve all violations which (theoretically) can be autocorrected in file " +
179→                    "${code.filePathOrStdin()} in $MAX_FORMAT_RUNS_PER_FILE consecutive runs of format."
180→            }
181→        }
182→
183→        errors
184→            .sortedWith { (l), (r) -> if (l.line != r.line) l.line - r.line else l.col - r.col }
185→            .forEach { (e, corrected) -> callback(e, corrected) }
186→
187→        if (!mutated && formatRunCount == 1) {
188→            return code.content
189→        }
190→
191→        val formattedCode =
192→            ruleExecutionContext
193→                .rootNode
194→                .text
195→                .replace("\n", ruleExecutionContext.determineLineSeparator(code.content))
196→        return if (hasUTF8BOM) {
197→            UTF8_BOM + formattedCode
198→        } else {
199→            formattedCode
200→        }.also {
201→            LOGGER.debug("Finished with formatting file '${code.fileNameOrStdin()}'")
202→        }
203→    }
204→
205→    private fun RuleExecutionContext.determineLineSeparator(fileContent: String): String {
206→        val eol = editorConfig[END_OF_LINE_PROPERTY]
207→        return when {
208→            eol == crlf || eol != lf && fileContent.lastIndexOf('\r') != -1 -> "\r\n"
209→            else -> "\n"
210→        }
211→    }
212→
213→    /**
214→     * Generates Kotlin `.editorconfig` file section content based on a path to a file or directory. Given that path, all '.editorconfig'
215→     * files on that path are taken into account to determine the values of properties which are already used.
216→     *
217→     * @return Kotlin section editorconfig content. For example:
218→     * ```properties
219→     * final-newline=true
220→     * indent-size=4
221→     * ```
222→     */
223→    public fun generateKotlinEditorConfigSection(filePath: Path): String {
224→        val codeStyle =
225→            editorConfigOverride
226→                .properties[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
227→                ?.parsed
228→                ?.safeAs<CodeStyleValue>()
229→                ?: CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
230→        val rules =
231→            ruleProviders
232→                .map { it.createNewRuleInstance() }
233→                .distinctBy { it.ruleId }
234→                .toSet()
235→        return EditorConfigGenerator(fileSystem, editorConfigLoaderEc4j)
236→            .generateEditorconfig(
237→                rules,
238→                codeStyle,
239→                filePath,
240→            )
241→    }
242→
243→    /**
244→     * Reduce memory usage by cleaning internal caches.
245→     */
246→    public fun trimMemory() {
247→        THREAD_SAFE_EDITOR_CONFIG_CACHE.clear()
248→    }
249→
250→    /**
251→     * Get the list of files which will be accessed by KtLint when linting or formatting the given file or directory.
252→     * The API consumer can use this list to observe changes in '.editorconfig' files. Whenever such a change is
253→     * observed, the API consumer should call [reloadEditorConfigFile].
254→     * To avoid unnecessary access to the file system, it is best to call this method only once for the root of the
255→     * project which is to be [lint] or [format].
256→     */
257→    public fun editorConfigFilePaths(path: Path): List<Path> = EditorConfigFinder(editorConfigLoaderEc4j).findEditorConfigs(path)
258→
259→    /**
260→     * Reloads an '.editorconfig' file given that it is currently loaded into the KtLint cache. This method is intended
261→     * to be called by the API consumer when it is aware of changes in the '.editorconfig' file that should be taken
262→     * into account with next calls to [lint] and/or [format]. See [editorConfigFilePaths] to get the list of
263→     * '.editorconfig' files which need to be observed.
264→     */
265→    public fun reloadEditorConfigFile(path: Path) {
266→        THREAD_SAFE_EDITOR_CONFIG_CACHE.reloadIfExists(
267→            Resource.Resources.ofPath(path, StandardCharsets.UTF_8),
268→        )
269→    }
270→
271→    public fun transformToAst(code: Code): FileASTNode = createRuleExecutionContext(this, code).rootNode
272→
273→    public companion object {
274→        internal const val UTF8_BOM = "\uFEFF"
275→
276→        public const val STDIN_FILE: String = "<stdin>"
277→
278→        private const val MAX_FORMAT_RUNS_PER_FILE = 3
279→    }
280→}

 50→ * When wrapping a rule from the ktlint project and modifying its behavior, please change the [ruleId] and [about] fields, so that it is
 51→ * clear to users whenever they used the original rule provided by KtLint versus a modified version which is not maintained by the KtLint
 52→ * project.

 57→     * 'standard' is reserved for rules which are maintained by the KtLint project. Rules created by custom rule set providers and API
 58→     * Consumers should use a prefix other than 'standard' to mark the origin of rules which are not maintained by the KtLint project.

137→     * should be disabled.

139→     * When called in [beforeFirstNode], no AST nodes will be visited. [afterLastNode] is still called.
140→     *
141→     * When called in [beforeVisitChildNodes], the child nodes of that node will not be visited. [afterVisitChildNodes]

 25→private val KOTLIN_PSI_FILE_FACTORY_PROVIDER = KotlinPsiFileFactoryProvider()
 26→
 27→private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()

 29→internal class RuleExecutionContext private constructor(
 30→    val code: Code,
 31→    val rootNode: FileASTNode,
 32→    val ruleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>,
 33→    val editorConfig: EditorConfig,
 34→    val positionInTextLocator: (offset: Int) -> LineAndColumn,
 35→) {
 36→    private lateinit var suppressionLocator: SuppressionLocator
 37→
 38→    init {
 39→        rebuildSuppressionLocator()
 40→    }
 41→
 42→    fun rebuildSuppressionLocator() {
 43→        suppressionLocator = SuppressionLocatorBuilder.buildSuppressedRegionsLocator(rootNode, editorConfig)
 44→    }
 45→
 46→    fun executeRule(
 47→        rule: Rule,
 48→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 49→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 50→    ) {
 51→        try {
 52→            rule.startTraversalOfAST()
 53→            rule.beforeFirstNode(
 54→                // The rule get access to an EditConfig which is filtered by the properties which are actually registered as being used by
 55→                // the rule. In this way it can be forced that the rule actually registers the properties that it uses and the field becomes
 56→                // reliable to be used by for example the ".editorconfig" file generator.
 57→                // Note that also the CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY is provided because that property is needed to determine the default value of an
 58→                // EditorConfigProperty that is not explicitly defined.
 59→                editorConfig.filterBy(rule.usesEditorConfigProperties.plus(CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY)),
 60→            )
 61→            this.executeRuleOnNodeRecursively(rootNode, rule, autoCorrect, emit)
 62→            rule.afterLastNode()
 63→        } catch (e: RuleExecutionException) {
 64→            throw KtLintRuleException(
 65→                e.line,
 66→                e.col,
 67→                e.rule.ruleId.value,
 68→                """
 69→                Rule '${e.rule.ruleId.value}' throws exception in file '${code.fileNameOrStdin()}' at position (${e.line}:${e.col})
 70→                   Rule maintainer: ${e.rule.about.maintainer}
 71→                   Issue tracker  : ${e.rule.about.issueTrackerUrl}
 72→                   Repository     : ${e.rule.about.repositoryUrl}
 73→                """.trimIndent(),
 74→                e.cause,
 75→            )
 76→        }
 77→    }
 78→
 79→    private fun executeRuleOnNodeRecursively(
 80→        node: ASTNode,
 81→        rule: Rule,
 82→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 83→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 84→    ) {
 85→        /**
 86→         * The [suppressionLocator] can be changed during each visit of node when running [KtLintRuleEngine.format]. So a new handler is to
 87→         * be built before visiting the nodes.
 88→         */
 89→        val suppressHandler = SuppressHandler(suppressionLocator, autoCorrect, emit)
 90→        if (rule.shouldContinueTraversalOfAST()) {
 91→            try {
 92→                suppressHandler.handle(node, rule.ruleId) { autoCorrect, emit ->
 93→                    rule.beforeVisitChildNodes(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 94→                }
 95→                if (rule.shouldContinueTraversalOfAST()) {
 96→                    node
 97→                        .getChildren(null)
 98→                        .forEach { childNode ->
 99→                            suppressHandler.handle(childNode, rule.ruleId) { autoCorrect, emit ->
100→                                this.executeRuleOnNodeRecursively(
101→                                    childNode,
102→                                    rule,
103→                                    autoCorrect,
104→                                    emit,
105→                                )
106→                            }
107→                        }
108→                }
109→                suppressHandler.handle(node, rule.ruleId) { autoCorrect, emit ->
110→                    rule.afterVisitChildNodes(node, autoCorrect, emit)
111→                }
112→            } catch (e: Exception) {
113→                if (autoCorrect) {
114→                    // line/col cannot be reliably mapped as exception might originate from a node not present in the
115→                    // original AST
116→                    throw RuleExecutionException(
117→                        rule,
118→                        0,
119→                        0,
120→                        // Prevent extreme long stack trace caused by recursive call and only pass root cause
121→                        e.cause ?: e,
122→                    )
123→                } else {
124→                    val (line, col) = positionInTextLocator(node.startOffset)
125→                    throw RuleExecutionException(
126→                        rule,
127→                        line,
128→                        col,
129→                        // Prevent extreme long stack trace caused by recursive call and only pass root cause
130→                        e.cause ?: e,
131→                    )
132→                }
133→            }
134→        }
135→    }
136→
137→    companion object {
138→        internal fun createRuleExecutionContext(
139→            ktLintRuleEngine: KtLintRuleEngine,
140→            code: Code,
141→        ): RuleExecutionContext {
142→            val psiFileFactory = KOTLIN_PSI_FILE_FACTORY_PROVIDER.getKotlinPsiFileFactory(ktLintRuleEngine)
143→            val normalizedText = normalizeText(code.content)
144→            val positionInTextLocator = buildPositionInTextLocator(normalizedText)
145→
146→            val psiFileName =
147→                code
148→                    .filePath
149→                    ?.pathString
150→                    ?: if (code.script) {
151→                        "File.kts"
152→                    } else {
153→                        "File.kt"
154→                    }
155→            val psiFile =
156→                psiFileFactory.createFileFromText(
157→                    psiFileName,
158→                    KotlinLanguage.INSTANCE,
159→                    normalizedText,
160→                ) as KtFile
161→            psiFile
162→                .findErrorElement()
163→                ?.let { errorElement ->
164→                    val (line, col) = positionInTextLocator(errorElement.textOffset)
165→                    throw KtLintParseException(line, col, errorElement.errorDescription)
166→                }
167→
168→            val rootNode = psiFile.node
169→
170→            val editorConfig =
171→                ktLintRuleEngine
172→                    .editorConfigLoader
173→                    .load(code.filePath)
174→                    .also {
175→                        // TODO: Remove warning below in KtLint 0.52 or later as some users skips multiple versions
176→                        it.warnIfPropertyIsObsolete("disabled_rules", "0.49")
177→                        // TODO: Remove warning below in KtLint 0.52 or later as some users skips multiple versions
178→                        it.warnIfPropertyIsObsolete("ktlint_disabled_rules", "0.49")
179→                    }
180→
181→            val ruleProviders =
182→                ktLintRuleEngine
183→                    .ruleProviders(
184→                        RuleExecutionRuleFilter(editorConfig),
185→                        RunAfterRuleFilter(),
186→                    )
187→
188→            return RuleExecutionContext(
189→                code,
190→                rootNode,
191→                ruleProviders,
192→                editorConfig,
193→                positionInTextLocator,
194→            )
195→        }
196→
197→        private fun normalizeText(text: String): String {
198→            return text
199→                .replace("\r\n", "\n")
200→                .replace("\r", "\n")
201→                .replaceFirst(UTF8_BOM, "")
202→        }
203→
204→        private fun PsiElement.findErrorElement(): PsiErrorElement? {
205→            if (this is PsiErrorElement) {
206→                return this
207→            }
208→            this.children.forEach { child ->
209→                val errorElement = child.findErrorElement()
210→                if (errorElement != null) {
211→                    return errorElement
212→                }
213→            }
214→            return null
215→        }
216→    }
217→}
218→
219→private fun EditorConfig.warnIfPropertyIsObsolete(
220→    propertyName: String,
221→    ktlintVersion: String,
222→) {
223→    if (this.contains(propertyName)) {
224→        LOGGER.warn {
225→            "Editorconfig property '$propertyName' is obsolete and is not used by KtLint starting from version $ktlintVersion. Remove " +
226→                "the property from all '.editorconfig' files."
227→        }
228→    }
229→}
230→
231→private class RuleExecutionException(
232→    val rule: Rule,
233→    val line: Int,
234→    val col: Int,
235→    override val cause: Throwable,
236→) : Throwable(cause)

  7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.CodeStyleValue

  9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfig
 10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfigProperty
 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.RULE_EXECUTION_PROPERTY_TYPE
 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.RuleExecution
 13→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.ktLintRuleExecutionPropertyName
 14→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.ktLintRuleSetExecutionPropertyName
 15→
 16→/**
 17→ * Filters the [RuleProvider]s defined in the [KtLintRuleEngine] for [Rule]s which are enabled for the given [EditorConfig].
 18→ */
 19→internal class RuleExecutionRuleFilter(
 20→    private val editorConfig: EditorConfig,
 21→) : RuleFilter {
 22→    override fun filter(ruleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>): Set<RuleProvider> {
 23→        val ruleExecutionFilter =
 24→            RuleExecutionFilter(
 25→                editorConfig.ruleExecutionProperties(ruleProviders),
 26→                editorConfig[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 27→            )
 28→        return ruleProviders
 29→            .filter { ruleExecutionFilter.isEnabled(it) }
 30→            .toSet()
 31→    }
 32→
 33→    private fun EditorConfig.ruleExecutionProperties(ruleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>): Map<String, RuleExecution> {
 34→        val ruleExecutionPropertyNames =
 35→            ruleExecutionPropertyNames(ruleProviders)
 36→                .plus(ruleSetExecutionPropertyNames(ruleProviders))
 37→                .plus(EXPERIMENTAL_RULES_EXECUTION_PROPERTY.name)
 38→        return map { it.name to RULE_EXECUTION_PROPERTY_TYPE.parse(it.sourceValue).parsed }
 39→            .toMap()
 40→            .filterValues { it != null }
 41→            .filterKeys { it in ruleExecutionPropertyNames }
 42→    }
 43→
 44→    private fun ruleExecutionPropertyNames(ruleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>) =
 45→        ruleProviders
 46→            .map { it.ruleId.ktLintRuleExecutionPropertyName() }
 47→            .distinct()
 48→
 49→    private fun ruleSetExecutionPropertyNames(ruleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>) =
 50→        ruleProviders
 51→            .map { it.ruleId.ruleSetId.ktLintRuleSetExecutionPropertyName() }
 52→            .distinct()
 53→}
 54→
 55→/**
 56→ * Creates a filter of [RuleExecution] properties. This filter is basically an extract of the [EditorConfig] which allows to retrieve rule
 57→ * execution properties for which no proper [EditorConfigProperty] is defined as rules have no need to be able to extract such a property
 58→ * from [EditorConfig].
 59→ */
 60→private class RuleExecutionFilter(
 61→    val ruleExecutionProperties: Map<String, RuleExecution>,
 62→    val codeStyleValue: CodeStyleValue,
 63→) {
 64→    fun isEnabled(ruleProvider: RuleProvider) = isRuleEnabled(ruleProvider.createNewRuleInstance())
 65→
 66→    private fun isRuleEnabled(rule: Rule) =
 67→        /*
 68→         * If set for the rule, the rule execution property takes precedence above other checks. This allows for execution of a specific
 69→         * experimental or ktlint_official code style rule without enabling them all. Also, this allows to disable a specific rule in case
 70→         * the experimental and/or ktlint_official code style rules are enabled.
 71→         */
 72→        ruleExecution(rule.ruleId.ktLintRuleExecutionPropertyName())
 73→            ?.let { it == RuleExecution.enabled }
 74→            ?: isRuleConditionallyEnabled(rule)
 75→
 76→    private fun isRuleConditionallyEnabled(rule: Rule) =
 77→        when {
 78→            rule is Rule.Experimental && rule is Rule.OfficialCodeStyle ->
 79→                isExperimentalEnabled(rule) && isOfficialCodeStyleEnabled(rule)
 80→            rule is Rule.Experimental ->
 81→                isExperimentalEnabled(rule)
 82→            rule is Rule.OfficialCodeStyle ->
 83→                isOfficialCodeStyleEnabled(rule)
 84→            else ->
 85→                isRuleSetEnabled(rule)
 86→        }
 87→
 88→    private fun isExperimentalEnabled(rule: Rule) =
 89→        ruleExecution(EXPERIMENTAL_RULES_EXECUTION_PROPERTY.name) == RuleExecution.enabled &&
 90→            ruleExecution(rule.ruleId.ruleSetId.ktLintRuleSetExecutionPropertyName()) != RuleExecution.disabled &&
 91→            ruleExecution(rule.ruleId.ktLintRuleExecutionPropertyName()) != RuleExecution.disabled
 92→
 93→    private fun isOfficialCodeStyleEnabled(rule: Rule) =
 94→        codeStyleValue == CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official &&
 95→            ruleExecution(rule.ruleId.ruleSetId.ktLintRuleSetExecutionPropertyName()) != RuleExecution.disabled &&
 96→            ruleExecution(rule.ruleId.ktLintRuleExecutionPropertyName()) != RuleExecution.disabled
 97→
 98→    private fun isRuleSetEnabled(rule: Rule) =
 99→        ruleExecution(rule.ruleId.ruleSetId.ktLintRuleSetExecutionPropertyName())
100→            .let { ruleSetExecution ->
101→                if (ruleSetExecution?.name == EXPERIMENTAL_RULES_EXECUTION_PROPERTY.name) {
102→                    // Rules in the experimental rule set are only run when enabled explicitly.
103→                    ruleSetExecution == RuleExecution.enabled
104→                } else {
105→                    // Rules in other rule sets are enabled by default.
106→                    ruleSetExecution != RuleExecution.disabled
107→                }
108→            }
109→
110→    private fun ruleExecution(ruleExecutionPropertyName: String) = ruleExecutionProperties[ruleExecutionPropertyName]
111→}

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.internal

 3→/**
 4→ * Provides policy to have consistent and restricted `id` field naming style.
 5→ */
 6→internal object IdNamingPolicy {
 7→    private const val SIMPLE_ID_REGEX = "[a-z]+(-[a-z]+)*"
 8→    private val RULE_ID_REGEX = "$SIMPLE_ID_REGEX:$SIMPLE_ID_REGEX".toRegex()
 9→    private val RULE_SET_ID_REGEX = SIMPLE_ID_REGEX.toRegex()
10→
11→    /**
12→     * Checks provided [ruleId] is valid.
13→     *
14→     * Will throw [IllegalArgumentException] on invalid [ruleId] name.
15→     */
16→    internal fun enforceRuleIdNaming(ruleId: String) =
17→        require(ruleId.matches(RULE_ID_REGEX)) { "Rule with id '$ruleId' must match regexp '${RULE_ID_REGEX.pattern}'" }
18→
19→    /**
20→     * Checks provided [ruleSetId] is valid.
21→     *
22→     * Will throw [IllegalArgumentException] on invalid [ruleSetId] name.
23→     */
24→    internal fun enforceRuleSetIdNaming(ruleSetId: String) =
25→        require(ruleSetId.matches(RULE_SET_ID_REGEX)) { "Rule set id '$ruleSetId' must match '${RULE_SET_ID_REGEX.pattern}'" }
26→}
